topic: China SG Network null — 2009 eclipse coverage precision rewrite (EXP-349 integration)
change_summary: EXP-349 integration: replaced the loose "coverage approaching 100% along the path" sentence in WIN-014.detail_evidence paragraph 3 with ~245-word precise totality-corridor geography (Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai, ~258 km width) plus two structural rebuttals to the off-path partial-coverage defender pivot (no-published-coverage-threshold + coverage-scaling-arithmetic-survives).
our_argument_summary: The dome cites the 2009 Chinese SG-network null as confirming the model. Applying the dome's own coupling κ=1.677 nT/µGal to the documented 5–15 nT eclipse ΔB predicts a 3–9 µGal contact-phase pulse. The SG network recorded 0 µGal across all sites — refuted by data. Carve-outs that exempt 2009 also destroy PRED-TIER-3.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome publishes κ = 1.677 nT/µGal as a constant. WIN-014 entry in 02-wins.txt is a one-line claim citing "Sun 2010" with "0.0 uGal across network. Replicates WIN-013." No derivation path from dome geometry to either the ΔB or the Δg predicted value; the dome treats the null as confirmation rather than producing the prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No live computation: WIN-014 in monitor.py raw-text snapshot is absent (no monitor.py file in raw-text/); the 2026-04 curmudgeon cycles found no monitor.py domain for WIN-014. The claim is a static text assertion, not a code-validated check.
data_sources_used: Sun et al. 2010 (cited by dome; closest verified record is 2013 CJG doi:10.6038/cjg20130306)
Kim & Chang 2018 (cited by our review, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome publishes κ without a coverage threshold; the new prose explicitly notes that any post-hoc coverage-gating would be a scope condition the dome would have had to publish in advance.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by EXP-349. The patch is a prose-only edit to WIN-014.detail_evidence; no code, monitor.py, or pull_data.py claims modified.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py / pull_data.py exists in raw-text/ snapshot (only 8 .txt files mirroring static site sections). WIN-014 in raw-text/02-wins.txt is a single line: "WIN-014 — China SG Network Null (2009 Eclipse) CONFIRMED  Source: Sun 2010. 0.0 uGal across network. Replicates WIN-013." No fetch, no comparison logic.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No standard-physics analog: there is no orthodox prediction that the 2009 Chinese SG network should have observed an eclipse-induced gravity pulse; the dome's claim is a novel coupling, not a renamed standard mechanism. Refutation runs through the dome's OWN published κ, not through standard physics displacement.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 2009 measurement was published by Sun 2010 / the 2013 CJG record well before the dome adopted it as "confirming." The dome reads a null measurement as confirmation by treating its own model as predicting zero — the prediction is post-hoc relative to the observation.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome geometry (disc radius, firmament height) to the 2009 zero-result is offered by the dome. The dome's κ value (WIN-012) is itself reverse-engineered from BOU 2017, not derived; WIN-014 inherits this non-derivation.

holes_found.description: The new prose pivots entirely to rebutting the off-totality-stations pivot but never establishes that ANY of the eight SG sites were actually in or near the totality corridor. The argument as written reads as if all eight gravimeters were off-path ("the cited Chinese SG campaign's eight gravimeters were not necessarily uniformly on that path") and then defends the κ-coupling case under that conservative concession via the 70% scaling math. But the stronger case — that the Yangtze-valley corridor passes through Wuhan and that Wuhan-area SG instrumentation almost certainly contributed to the 2009 dataset — gets no explicit naming. A defender can read this paragraph and credibly object: "You've only rebutted me under a worst-case site distribution. Show me a single SG site that was actually on or near the path of totality and you'd have the direct 5–15 nT → 3–9 µGal refutation without needing the 70%-scaling workaround." The structural argument is sound but the rhetorical move concedes more than necessary, weakening the verdict's perceived strength on first reading.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence before or after the "not necessarily uniformly on that path" hedge naming at least one in-corridor SG-instrumented city (Wuhan is the highest-confidence candidate — it sits on the path of totality and is a long-established SG site). Phrasing: "At least one of the cited SG sites (Wuhan, on the Yangtze-valley path of totality) experienced the full eclipse, against which the direct 5–15 nT → 3–9 µGal prediction applies without any coverage-scaling adjustment; the off-totality argument below extends the refutation to the remaining sites." If the per-site list is not independently verifiable, soften: "Of the eight gravimeters, the Yangtze-valley sites (Wuhan and nearby) were on the central path and the high-latitude sites were off; the structural argument below covers both regimes."

holes_found.description: The 70% partial-coverage anchor is rhetorically convenient but not motivated by the actual coverage distribution of the four named off-totality cities (Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi). Of those, only Beijing (~75%) and Changchun (~70%) are near 70%. Lhasa is geographically the nearest of the four to the path (the 2009 eclipse entered China through Bhutan/Tibet) and had perhaps 50–60% coverage. Urumqi is ~2500 km from the path in Xinjiang and likely received well under 40% coverage. At 30% linear scaling, ΔB falls to 1.5–4.5 nT, Δg = 0.9–2.7 µGal — right at the SG floor of 0.1–1 µGal. A defender can cherry-pick the lowest-coverage sites in the deployment and argue the network-wide null is consistent with κ at the actual site coverages. The 70% number works for the moderate-latitude sites but not for the westernmost ones; the prose doesn't engage that asymmetry.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) bracket the scaling with a low-end case: "even at 50% coverage, the predicted Δg is 1.5–4.5 µGal, still 1.5× to 45× above the 0.1–1 µGal floor; only sites at coverages below ~30% would fall to the floor, and the campaign's mid-latitude stations (Beijing, Changchun) were well above that threshold" — or (b) lean harder on the network-wide aspect: "the 70%-coverage calculation is a worst-case anchor for the mid-latitude sites (Beijing, Changchun, etc.); any post-hoc coverage threshold that exempts even those sites is incompatible with κ's published status as a constant." The current single-anchor framing leaves the lowest-coverage sites as an unaddressed escape valve.

holes_found.description: The phrase "the network-wide null thus refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling" asserts robustness across scaling forms but the prose body only computes the linear case. EXP-349's defender_pivots_addressed explicitly worked the quadratic case (0.7² = 0.49 → ΔB 2.45–7.35 nT → Δg 1.5–4.4 µGal, still 1.5× to 44× above floor) but that work is not in the published prose. A defender could plausibly argue ΔB scales as (obscuration)^n for some n > 1, since the eclipse-magnetic literature does not pin down the scaling exponent. Without the quadratic case in print, "any plausible coverage scaling" is doing rhetorical work the prose doesn't fully back.
holes_found.recommendation: Append a parenthetical after "any plausible coverage scaling": "(quadratic scaling — 0.7² ≈ 0.49 — produces ΔB 2.5–7.4 nT and Δg ≈ 1.5–4.4 µGal, still well above the floor; the argument fails only at scaling exponents implausibly steeper than the eclipse-magnetic literature reports)". This makes the robustness claim auditable rather than asserted.

holes_found.description: The vise argument ("any coverage threshold consistent with exempting 2009 also exempts much of the 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble [Kim & Chang 2018] the dome itself invokes for WIN-068") is structurally correct but unanchored in the actual Kim & Chang ensemble composition. The 207 magnetic measurements span observatories at varying distances from each total-eclipse path, and a defender could claim WIN-068 invokes only the near-totality subset. The vise bites only if the median coverage in the Kim & Chang sample is high enough that the same threshold that exempts 2009 also exempts those measurements; the prose asserts this without showing the distribution.
holes_found.recommendation: Either tighten with a number ("Kim & Chang 2018's 207 measurements have a median observatory-to-path distance of X; a coverage threshold exempting Beijing/Changchun in 2009 would exempt Y% of that ensemble") or hedge slightly ("a coverage threshold high enough to exempt the off-totality 2009 stations would also exclude a substantial fraction of the 1991–2016 ensemble Kim & Chang 2018 invoke"). Current absolute phrasing ("also exempts much of") invites a defender to demand the numerator.

holes_found.description: Listing "Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi" together as "substantial partial rather than totality" is broadly correct but geographically smears very different coverage regimes. Beijing/Changchun are mid-latitude (~70–75% obscuration). Lhasa sits closer to the path entry through Bhutan/Tibet (~50–60%). Urumqi is far northwest in Xinjiang and likely below 40%. Lumping them obscures the asymmetry the prose then has to defend against (hole #2 above).
holes_found.recommendation: Either split the list into tiers ("sites at moderate partial coverage (Beijing ~75%, Changchun ~70%) and at low partial coverage (Lhasa ~50–60%, Urumqi <40%)") or trim to two examples plus an "and others" formulation. The verifiable per-site obscuration numbers exist in NASA's 2009 July 22 eclipse path tables and would make the prose checkable.

stronger_arguments.description: The strongest single move would be to name the SG site closest to the totality corridor and run the direct (un-scaled) calculation: 5–15 nT → 3–9 µGal at that site, vs the recorded 0 µGal. This collapses the rebuttal to one sentence and bypasses the entire off-path coverage-scaling apparatus. Wuhan, on the Yangtze valley near the eclipse central line, is the obvious candidate; if a publicly verifiable Wuhan SG record exists, citing it directly would be a higher-value edit than tightening the structural argument.
sources: Wuhan Geodynamics Observatory SG records (if accessible)
Sun et al. 2010 / 2013 CJG paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) per-site coverage table

stronger_arguments.description: A second strong move: explicitly compute the predicted-vs-measured envelope at the lowest plausible coverage (~30% at Urumqi-type sites) and show even there the prediction (~0.9–2.7 µGal) sits inside the SG measurement window. This forecloses the cherry-picked-low-coverage escape valve that hole #2 flags.
sources: NASA 2009 July 22 path tables (per-site coverage maps)
Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013 (doi:10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101) for SG noise-floor characterization

kernel_of_truth.description: The patch correctly acknowledges what ISS-1959 surfaced: the eight Chinese SG sites were not uniformly on the path of totality, and the earlier "approaching 100% along the path" prose invited the misread that they were. Naming the path geography precisely (Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai corridor, ~258 km width — verifiable against NASA 2009 July 22 bulletins) and admitting the per-site coverage table is not in English-language indices (data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence ¶3) is the right credibility move. The off-totality-rebuttal apparatus the patch adds (no published coverage threshold + arithmetic survives 70% scaling) is structurally a valid response to the defender pivot ISS-1959 raised, and the math (3.5–10.5 nT through κ=1.677 → 2.1–6.3 µGal; verified arithmetically this run) is correct.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel-of-truth (non-uniform SG site coverage) is precision, not exoneration. The verdict load-bears on the dome's own κ being published as a constant (data/wins.json:WIN-012, raw-text/03-predictions.txt PRED-TIER-3 invocation) — and as data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence ¶4 notes, the carve-outs that would exempt 2009 from κ all destroy the August 2026 PRED-TIER-3 derivation. The structural argument the patch adds doesn't weaken when site coverage varies; it strengthens, because the dome would have to publish a coverage-scaling function in advance to claim any partial-coverage exemption, and the dome has not done so (no mention of coverage-gating in raw-text/05-model.txt or raw-text/03-predictions.txt for κ). The verdict (Refuted by Data) is unchanged by the patch and is correctly anchored on the empirical SG null, not on the structural meta-argument.

deeper_analysis:
The EXP-349 patch correctly closes ISS-1959 (the c7 H4 moderate hole on imprecise corridor geography + off-path defender pivot). I verified all the patch's arithmetic this run: 3.5/1.677=2.087≈2.1 µGal, 10.5/1.677=6.260≈6.3 µGal, 6.3/0.1=63× ≈ "60×", 2.1/1.0=2.1× ≈ "2×". The corridor cities (Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai) and ~258 km totality width match NASA's 22 July 2009 eclipse bulletins. The patch's honesty about the per-site coverage gap ("we have not located a per-site coverage table in English") is the right credibility move and is preferable to making up a citation. The verdict (Refuted by Data) is unchanged and remains correctly classified.

That said, the patch concedes more rhetorical ground than necessary. The strongest version of WIN-014 names at least one in-corridor SG site (Wuhan is the obvious candidate — Yangtze-valley path of totality, established SG instrumentation) and applies the direct (un-scaled) 3–9 µGal prediction to it. The patch chose to defend the all-sites-off-path worst case, which is mathematically robust but rhetorically gives away the easier win. Holes #1 and #2 above are the recommended tightening. Holes #3–#5 are minor cosmetic improvements.

The verdict-text framing (Refuted by Data vs Self-Contradicted) is unchanged by EXP-349 and remains sound: the empirical SG-null observation is the load-bearing premise, not a dome-derivation step. WIN-012 (κ definition) stays Self-Contradicted; WIN-013/WIN-014 stay Refuted by Data. No verdict-change indicated.

No carry-forward from prior cycles: the c7 H4 hole this patch closes is the target of EXP-349 itself; c8/c9 were patch-integration verification (clean); c10 raised a minor TLDR phrasing issue ("eight-gravimeter") that EXP-349 does not interact with — the TLDRs already read "approximately eight superconducting/spring gravimeters across roughly six sites" which is the correct softened form. Unresolved_prior_cycle is empty.

citations_verified: NASA 22 July 2009 eclipse path width 258 km (max central duration) — verifiable against NASA Eclipse Bulletin for that date
Corridor cities Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai are on the 2009 path of totality (verifiable against NASA path tables)
κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is the dome-published value (consistent with data/wins.json:WIN-012 and PRED-TIER-3 invocation in raw-text/03-predictions.txt)
Arithmetic: 3.5/1.677=2.087, 10.5/1.677=6.260, both round to patch's stated 2.1–6.3 µGal
citations_unchecked: Kim & Chang 2018 DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — flagged historically (c7 H1), but c8/c9/c10 did not re-raise, treating as resolved; not re-verified this run
Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013 DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 — accepted as consistent with the well-known Reports on Progress in Physics SG review
2013 CJG record doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 — Chinese-language paper, primary-source verification not attempted
Sun et al. 2010 (dome-cited) — not independently verifiable in English-language indices, as the patch acknowledges

summary_for_decider: EXP-349 patch correctly closes ISS-1959 (c7 H4 moderate) — all the patch's structural claims and arithmetic verify. Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds, confidence 0.88. Two moderate fresh holes (H1, H2) and three minor (H3–H5) introduced by the new prose itself — recommend minor_edit to add a named in-corridor SG site (e.g., Wuhan) and bracket the coverage-scaling math at the low-coverage end. Unresolved_prior_cycle empty. No carry-over from c8/c9/c10. Suitable for queue follow-up (EXP or self-applied patch addressing H1 + H2 as a paired tightening; H3–H5 can ride along or be deferred).
